edits.slides.4.label: User Flow Mapping
edits.slides.4.title: Mapping the flow before touching the design
edits.slides.4.content: Before sketching anything, I mapped the full RX→Scan→View sequence as users actually experienced it. That map made the friction clusters impossible to ignore.
edits.slides.4.issues.0: RX setup interrupted the clinician before a single scan could begin
edits.slides.4.issues.1: Tool access forced a physical break at the peak of scan rhythm
edits.slides.4.issues.2: No completion signal — View felt like a question, not an answer
edits.slides.4.highlight: The friction wasn't random. It lived at every handoff between phases.
